synopsis: The Standing Judicial Commission reversed the Korean Capital Presbytery's censure of Deacon Samuel Hong, finding that the Presbytery lacked original jurisdiction over communicant members and should have been tried in the Session. The court held that regardless of the merits of the underlying charges (contumacy and making false statements), the procedural violation of jurisdiction was fatal to the action.
notes: Decided December 5, 2001 at McLean Presbyterian Church, McLean, VA. Panel of five members. One dissent (RE Howard Donahoe). Vote was 16 concur, 1 dissent, 1 recused, 1 disqualified, 5 absent. Reasoning and Opinion written by TE Jim Smith with concurrence by TE Charles McGowan, RE Grant McCabe, RE John White and RE Jack Williamson. Statement of Facts prepared by RE John White.
